verses.latin_vulgate: Exaltabo te, Domine, quoniam suscepisti me,  nec delectasti inimicos meos super me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲרוֹמִמְךָ֣ יְ֭הֹוָה כִּ֣י דִלִּיתָ֑נִי וְלֹֽא־שִׂמַּ֖חְתָּ אֹיְבַ֣י לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I will extol thee, O Lord; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine Deus meus, clamavi ad te, et sanasti me.
verses.king_james: O Lord my God, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, eduxisti ab inferno animam meam ;  salvasti me a descendentibus in lacum.
verses.king_james: O Lord, thou hast brought up my soul from the grave: thou hast kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Psallite Domino, sancti ejus ;  et confitemini memoriæ sanctitatis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זַמְּר֣וּ לַיהֹוָ֣ה חֲסִידָ֑יו וְ֝הוֹד֗וּ לְזֵ֣כֶר קׇדְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Sing unto the Lord, O ye saints of his, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam ira in indignatione ejus,  et vita in voluntate ejus :  ad vesperum demorabitur fletus,  et ad matutinum lætitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י רֶ֨גַע ׀ בְּאַפּוֹ֮ חַיִּ֢ים בִּרְצ֫וֹנ֥וֹ בָּ֭עֶרֶב יָלִ֥ין בֶּ֗כִי וְלַבֹּ֥קֶר רִנָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: For his anger endureth but a moment; in his favour is life: weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem dixi in abundantia mea :  Non movebor in æternum.
verses.king_james: And in my prosperity I said, I shall never be moved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, in voluntate tua præstitisti decori meo virtutem ;  avertisti faciem tuam a me, et factus sum conturbatus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְֽהֹוָ֗ה בִּרְצוֹנְךָ֮ הֶעֱמַ֢דְתָּה לְֽהַרְרִ֫י־עֹ֥ז הִסְתַּ֥רְתָּ פָנֶ֗יךָ הָיִ֥יתִי נִבְהָֽל׃
verses.king_james: Lord, by thy favour thou hast made my mountain to stand strong: thou didst hide thy face, and I was troubled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad te, Domine, clamabo,  et ad Deum meum deprecabor.
verses.king_james: I cried to thee, O Lord; and unto the Lord I made supplication.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ utilitas in sanguine meo,  dum descendo in corruptionem ?  numquid confitebitur tibi pulvis,  aut annuntiabit veritatem tuam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־בֶּ֥צַע בְּדָמִי֮ בְּרִדְתִּ֢י אֶ֫ל־שָׁ֥חַת הֲיוֹדְךָ֥ עָפָ֑ר הֲיַגִּ֥יד אֲמִתֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: What profit is there in my blood, when I go down to the pit? Shall the dust praise thee? shall it declare thy truth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivit Dominus, et misertus est mei ;  Dominus factus est adjutor meus.
verses.king_james: Hear, O Lord, and have mercy upon me: Lord, be thou my helper.

verses.latin_vulgate: Convertisti planctum meum in gaudium mihi ;  conscidisti saccum meum, et circumdedisti me lætitia :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָפַ֣כְתָּ מִסְפְּדִי֮ לְמָח֢וֹל לִ֥֫י פִּתַּ֥חְתָּ שַׂקִּ֑י וַֽתְּאַזְּרֵ֥נִי שִׂמְחָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Thou hast turned for me my mourning into dancing: thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut cantet tibi gloria mea, et non compungar.  Domine Deus meus, in æternum confitebor tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְמַ֤עַן ׀ יְזַמֶּרְךָ֣ כָ֭בוֹד וְלֹ֣א יִדֹּ֑ם יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱ֝לֹהַ֗י לְעוֹלָ֥ם אוֹדֶֽךָּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: To the end that my glory may sing praise to thee, and not be silent. O Lord my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.
